title: CDX Content Anchor and Person Definitions
description: Reusable schema definitions for ContentAnchor, ContentAnchorUri, and Person, used by collaboration, phantoms, security, and other extensions.

contentHash.description: Algorithm-prefixed content digest in 'algorithm:hexdigest' form. The hex length is fixed per algorithm (sha256/sha3-256/blake3 = 64, sha384 = 96, sha512/sha3-512 = 128). Shared definition for every content-hash field; do not inline a hash pattern elsewhere.

base64.description: Base64-encoded binary value (standard or URL-safe alphabet, optional padding). Shared definition for base64 fields; do not inline a base64 pattern elsewhere. Multi-line PEM (certificates) is NOT covered by this.

relativePath.description: Archive-relative file path: forward-slash separated, no leading slash, no '..'/'.' traversal segments (Zip-Slip safe). Restricted to ASCII [A-Za-z0-9._-] for portability; note the container spec MUSTs UTF-8 but only SHOULDs URL-safe characters, so this is intentionally stricter. Shared definition; do not inline a path pattern elsewhere.

mimeType.description: Media type in lowercase 'type/subtype' form (canonical, no parameters). Shared definition; do not inline a MIME pattern elsewhere.

person.description: Base person/agent identity. Extensions may add additional properties via allOf composition.

identifier.description: Persistent identifier (ORCID, DID, URL, institutional ID). For scholarly documents, ORCID format is recommended: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1825-0097

requiredSigner.description: One member of a document's required-signer set (Security Extension §3.12). It names a credential a verifier MUST find a valid signature from, in AUTHENTICATED terms — never an advisory signer field. Exactly one identity kind, each mapping to one credential path: `did` (the keyId path's `kid`, compared fragment-stripped and byte-for-byte), `x5tS256` (the X.509 path's leaf-certificate `x5t#S256` thumbprint), or `jkt` (the WebAuthn path's RFC 7638 public-key thumbprint). A required signer is NOT a trust anchor: the verifier's own pin still decides trust, so an entry naming an unpinned credential can never be satisfied.

did.description: keyId path: the DID (did:key, did:jwk or did:web) matched against a signature's signed `kid`, compared fragment-stripped and byte-for-byte. No fragment in the entry itself.

x5tS256.description: X.509 path: the base64url SHA-256 thumbprint of the signing leaf certificate's DER, matched against the signature's signed `x5t#S256`.

jkt.description: WebAuthn path: the base64url RFC 7638 SHA-256 thumbprint of the credential's COSE public key (as a JWK), matched against the thumbprint of a WebAuthn signature's `publicKey`.

signaturePolicy.description: Document signature policy (Security Extension §3.12). Declares the required-signer set that binds the signature set against stripping and downgrade. Carried in the manifest and bound by the signed manifest projection (§9.7), so any manifest-covering signature attests it. Author-asserted: a document that declares no policy has no set integrity, and a verifier MUST warn on a frozen or published document that declares none.

requiredSigners.description: The credentials that MUST each have a valid signature present. A verifier rejects a frozen or published document if any required signer is unsatisfied (stripped or downgraded). Order is not significant; entries are unique.

contentAnchor.description: Structured content anchor referencing a block, point, or range within document content.

blockId.description: Target block ID or named anchor ID

contentHash.description: Hash of the target block's text content at anchor creation time, for stale detection

contentAnchorUri.description: Content Anchor URI string for inline references. Format: #blockId, #blockId/offset, or #blockId/start-end
